title: Odds-Movement Prediction: predicting the market, not the match

config.target: sign(consensus favourite de-vigged prob: close - T); favourite fixed at T
config.model: logistic regression (numpy, L2=2.0), strict expanding walk-forward by kickoff time

test_b_true_close_subset.note: true close = last snapshot within 6h of kickoff; n is tiny => framework-only

test_c_pooled_momentum.interpretation: AUC<0.5 with CI entirely below 0.5 => recent up-moves are followed by DOWN-moves more often than chance (weak mean-reversion), not exploitable momentum

updrift_diagnostic.note: up-drift on ALL deep matches is an ARTIFACT of the truncated close (capture stopped 2026-06-23, before most kickoffs); on true closes it collapses to ~50/50.

data_caveat: Single odds source (theoddsapi), 1X2/h2h only, 68 deep intraday matches over ~12 days (2026-06-11..2026-06-23). The capture daemon stopped 2026-06-23, BEFORE most kickoffs, so for most matches the 'close' is the capture cutoff (median lead ~39.8h before KO), NOT a true settle; only 8 deep matches have a snapshot within 6h of kickoff. TEST A (n=68, one independent obs per match) is the honest primary test; TEST B (n=8) is framework-only; TEST C is autocorrelated within match so its AUC carries a block-bootstrap CI. The apparent open->close up-drift is a truncation artifact (it vanishes to ~50/50 on true closes). No order-book depth or matched volume; no Polymarket price history.
